reforms.description: In 1993, the New Zealand government established the New Zealand Qualifications Authority (NZQA) to develop a National Qualifications Framework aimed at improving the recognition of tertiary qualifications and enhancing flexibility in learning. Additionally, the government introduced a new system of industrial training through the Industry Act, establishing Industry Training Organisations (ITOs) to set standards for training qualifications in collaboration with the NZQA.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance the skills and qualifications of the workforce, which is essential for improving productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Both reforms were established and are currently in force, contributing to the development of human capital in New Zealand.
reforms.importance_rationale: Both reforms represent significant structural changes in the education and training system, aimed at improving the quality and recognition of qualifications.
reforms.source_quote: In order to improve the general recognition of tertiary qualifications as well as to try to break down the division between academic and vocational qualifications, a new government agency, the New Zealand Qualifications Authority (NZQA), was set up in 1993.
